title: Accenture
summary: Accenture had gone all-in as a Google Cloud partner and needed its on-prem stack moved to GCP. Migrated four applications — three Hadoop / Spark-Scala pipelines plus one with an ML layer — to Cloud Storage, PySpark, BigQuery and Cloud Composer. Terabytes of data, running natively on GCP.

beats.title: On-prem, four apps
beats.caption: Accenture had become a Google Cloud partner and needed its on-prem apps moved over. The stack: Hadoop clusters, Spark in Scala, Hive tables and Talend — four applications in all.

beats.title: Storage → Cloud Storage
beats.caption: First, the data itself: everything on HDFS lifted into Cloud Storage as the new landing layer.

beats.title: Spark Scala → PySpark
beats.caption: The heavy lifting: Spark jobs re-written from Scala to PySpark and run on Dataproc — refactored for maintainability along the way.

beats.title: Hive → BigQuery
beats.caption: The Hive tables became a BigQuery warehouse — same data, no cluster to babysit.

beats.title: Orchestrated on Cloud Composer
beats.caption: Finally, Talend's orchestration moved to Airflow on Cloud Composer. All four apps — three pipelines plus one with an ML layer — fully migrated and running natively on GCP.
